message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="yellow" summary="Agreeing on focus review timing, suggesting checklist item">
Steve, your point about reviewing a finished UI versus shaping it as it is built is the UX equivalent of Scott's feedback loop argument about domain types. Same principle, different layer: catch issues when they are cheap to fix, not after everything is built on top.

Your suggestion about making "where does focus go after each destructive action?" an explicit part of the template review step is practical and actionable. Right now the HTMX Focus Management Protocol exists in TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md, but it is not surfaced as a review prompt at the right moment. If we break features into smaller commits and the "routes + templates" commit triggers a review, the reviewer (especially Heydon, but also you) should have that question top of mind.

I would support adding a brief checklist item to the Atomic Green Step pipeline or the Definition of Done — something like: "For commits that add or modify templates with destructive actions, verify focus destination after removal." That would formalize what you are describing without adding ceremony.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Luca, yes — that's exactly the framing. Catching UX issues at the template layer is the same principle as catching domain modeling issues at the type layer: fix it before the next layer builds on top.

Your proposed checklist item wording is good: "For commits that add or modify templates with destructive actions, verify focus destination after removal." Short, specific, and actionable without adding ceremony. I'd support adding that to the Definition of Done under the Accessibility section, since it's closely related to the existing "Focus management is correct after HTMX swaps" item — it would make that existing item more concrete rather than adding a wholly new one.

That said, the retro is closed and we agreed on three action items. If the team wants to formalize this as a fourth item, we could raise it at the next retro or propose it as a small amendment. For now, the layer-by-layer commit process (Action Item 1) naturally creates the review moment where this question gets asked, so it may resolve itself.
input.summary: Support checklist item, fits existing DoD

routing.summary: Support checklist item, fits existing DoD

content.text: Responded to Luca. The retro is officially closed with 9/9 consensus on three action items. The follow-up conversations about focus checklist items and fragment swap patterns are good post-retro discussion, but no further action is needed from me right now. Ready for whatever the team works on next.
